message.content: crea un piano, nel folder dei piani da fare, che implementi questo use case tra la gestione dei materiali: prenda un documento da un libro (esempio questo file:  "/Users/roberdan/Downloads/EFFETTO SERRA_DA LEGGERE BENE!.pdf" e ne faccia una versione adatta allo studente, esempio questo file:  "/Users/roberdan/Downloads/Effetto serra.pdf" usando il maestro giusto o anche solo il coach e ne crei anche una mappa mentale . In pratica dato un contenuto mi deve creare una versione semplificata che ne spieghi i concetti essenziali, un riassunto quindi, una mappa mentale, una demo ed un quiz. In pratica dato un pezzo di libro su un argomento mi crei tutti i supporti che possano facilitare lo studente nel capirlo e studiarlo in autonomia. il tutto molto ben gestito da una bella e semplice UI. secondo me va integrato nella funzione materiali, ma pensaci bene tu. Fai un piano ben dettagliato e documentato che poi rivediamo insieme

content.thinking:
L'utente vuole che crei un piano dettagliato per implementare una nuova funzionalità in ConvergioEdu. La funzionalità deve:

1. Prendere un documento/PDF da un libro (contenuto originale complesso)
2. Creare una versione semplificata/adatta allo studente
3. Generare:
   - Riassunto con concetti essenziali
   - Mappa mentale
   - Demo
   - Quiz

L'obiettivo è trasformare materiale didattico complesso in supporti di studio accessibili per studenti con difficoltà di apprendimento.

Prima di creare il piano, devo:
1. Leggere i due PDF forniti per capire il delta tra contenuto originale e versione semplificata
2. Esplorare la codebase per capire come funziona la gestione materiali attuale
3. Capire i maestri disponibili e quale usare
4. Guardare le funzionalità esistenti (mappe mentali, quiz, ecc.)

Vado in plan mode per fare questo lavoro di esplorazione e poi scrivere il piano.

content.text:
Perfect! I have thoroughly explored the learning content generation features in ConvergioEdu. Here's a comprehensive analysis:

## LEARNING CONTENT GENERATION FEATURES

### 1. MIND MAPS (Mappe Mentali)

**Generation:**
- Handler: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/tools/handlers/mindmap-handler.ts`
- Creates hierarchical mind maps using MarkMap format with parentId-based relationships
- Converts flat node structure to nested markdown for rendering
- Nodes can be nested up to 6 heading levels deep

**Data Structure:**
```typescript
// src/types/tools.ts
MindmapData {
  title: string;      // Central topic
  nodes: MindmapNode[];
  markdown?: string;  // Generated markdown
}

MindmapNode {
  id: string;
  label: string;
  parentId?: string | null;  // null = branch from center
}
```

**Display Components:**
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/live-mindmap.tsx` - Real-time interactive mindmap with SSE event listening
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/interactive-markmap-renderer.tsx` - Interactive MarkMap renderer with node manipulation
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/education/knowledge-hub/renderers/mindmap-renderer.tsx` - Knowledge Hub wrapper

**Voice Commands:**
- Add node, connect nodes, expand node, delete node, focus node, set color
- Real-time updates via SSE (`/api/tools/sse`)

**Export Formats:**
- JSON, Markdown, SVG, PNG, PDF, FreeMind, XMind (in `/src/lib/tools/mindmap-export.ts`)

---

### 2. QUIZZES

**Generation:**
- Handler: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/tools/handlers/quiz-handler.ts`
- Creates multiple-choice quizzes with 4 options and correct answer indices
- Validates: at least 2 options, correctIndex within bounds

**Data Structure:**
```typescript
// src/types/tools.ts
QuizData {
  topic: string;
  questions: QuizQuestion[];
}

QuizQuestion {
  question: string;
  options: string[];
  correctIndex: number;  // 0-3
  explanation?: string;
}
```

**Display Components:**
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/quiz-tool.tsx` - Interactive quiz tool
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/education/knowledge-hub/renderers/quiz-renderer.tsx` - Knowledge Hub renderer with show/hide answers toggle

**Features:**
- Question validation
- Multiple choice with correct answer highlighting
- Detailed explanations for each answer

---

### 3. FLASHCARDS (FSRS Spaced Repetition)

**Generation:**
- Handler: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/tools/handlers/flashcard-handler.ts`
- Creates front/back flashcard pairs for spaced repetition learning

**Data Structure:**
```typescript
// src/types/tools.ts
FlashcardData {
  topic: string;
  cards: FlashcardItem[];
}

FlashcardItem {
  front: string;    // Question/prompt
  back: string;     // Answer (max 2000 chars)
}
```

**FSRS Algorithm:**
- Core implementation: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/education/fsrs.ts` (Line 2-420)
- Duolingo's FSRS-5 algorithm for optimal spacing
- Stability: How well the card is remembered (0.04 days to 1095 days / 3 years)
- Difficulty: How hard the card is (0-1, starts at 0.3)
- Retrievability: Probability of recall using power law decay formula
- Quality ratings: 1 = Again (forgot), 2 = Hard, 3 = Good, 4 = Easy
- Automatically adjusts next review interval based on performance

**Key Components:**
- `calculateRetrievability()` - Probability of recall based on elapsed time
- `reviewCard()` - Updates card after review with new stability/difficulty
- `calculateStats()` - Performance analytics (cards due, mastered, avg stability)
- `isDue()` - Check if card needs review

**Display Components:**
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/flashcard-tool.tsx`
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/education/flashcard.tsx`

---

### 4. SUMMARIES (Riassunti)

**Generation:**
- Handler: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/tools/handlers/summary-handler.ts`
- Creates structured summaries with sections and key points

**Data Structure:**
```typescript
// src/types/tools.ts
SummaryData {
  topic: string;
  sections: SummarySection[];
  length?: 'short' | 'medium' | 'long';
}

SummarySection {
  title: string;
  content: string;
  keyPoints?: string[];
}
```

**Display Components:**
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/summary-tool.tsx` - Wrapper with edit/view modes
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/summary-renderer.tsx` - Visual renderer
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/tools/summary-editor.tsx` - Inline editor
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/education/knowledge-hub/renderers/summary-renderer.tsx` - Knowledge Hub wrapper

**Features:**
- Edit mode for teacher/student modifications
- PDF export (generates styled HTML for print)
- Dynamic conversion to other formats

**Maieutic Method Support:**
- `open_student_summary` tool allows students to write their own summaries
- `student_summary_add_comment` allows maestros to add inline feedback on student text
- Structure: Intro → Main Content → Conclusion (with guiding questions)
- StudentSummaryData tracks comments, word count, modification history

---

### 5. AI-POWERED CONTENT TRANSFORMATION

**Conversation Summarization:**
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/ai/summarize.ts`
- `generateConversationSummary()` - Creates riassunto in Italian (max 200 words)
- `extractKeyFacts()` - Identifies decisions, preferences, learned concepts
- `extractTopics()` - Extracts main subjects discussed (max 5)
- `extractLearnings()` - Cross-session insights with confidence scores
  - Categories: preference, strength, weakness, interest, style

**Summary-to-Mindmap Conversion:**
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/tools/summary-export.ts`
- `convertSummaryToMindmap()` - Transforms summary sections into hierarchical mindmap nodes
- Root: summary topic → Branches: section titles → Sub-nodes: key points

**Summary-to-Flashcards Generation:**
- `generateFlashcardsFromSummary()` - Creates flashcard set from summary
- Generates cards from both main content and key points
- Front: Questions derived from section titles
- Back: Full content or key point text

**Learning Extraction:**
- LLM-powered extraction of student insights
- Confidence scoring (0.3-0.9)
- Automatic validation and filtering

---

### 6. TOOL CREATION & PERSISTENCE FLOW

**API Routes:**

1. **Creation:** `/api/tools/create` (POST)
   - Creates tool state and broadcasts `tool:created` event
   - Validates authentication and session ownership
   - Accepts: sessionId, maestroId, toolType, title, content

2. **Streaming:** `/api/tools/sse` (GET)
   - Server-Sent Events endpoint for real-time updates
   - Events: `tool:created`, `tool:update`, `tool:complete`, `tool:error`
   - Heartbeat mechanism every 30 seconds
   - Validates session ownership

3. **Modification:** `/api/tools/stream/modify` (POST)
   - Voice command modifications for mindmaps
   - Real-time synchronization across clients

**State Management:**
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/realtime/tool-state.ts` - In-memory tool state
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/realtime/tool-events.ts` - Event broadcasting system

**Tool Types Supported:**
All defined in `src/types/tools.ts`:
- mindmap, quiz, flashcard, summary, timeline, diagram, demo, search, formula, chart, webcam, pdf, homework

---

### 7. KNOWLEDGE HUB INTEGRATION

All tools integrate with Knowledge Hub for:
- Material storage and retrieval
- Search across all content types
- Smart collections with fuzzy search
- Timeline and calendar views
- Gallery view for visual materials

Renderers located at: `/src/components/education/knowledge-hub/renderers/`

---

### KEY FILES SUMMARY

| Feature | Core File | Type Definition | Handler |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|---------|
| Mindmap | mindmap-utils.ts | types/tools.ts | mindmap-handler.ts |
| Quiz | quiz-tool.tsx | types/tools.ts | quiz-handler.ts |
| Flashcard | fsrs.ts | types/index.ts | flashcard-handler.ts |
| Summary | summary-export.ts | types/tools.ts | summary-handler.ts |
| Extraction | summarize.ts | types/index.ts | (LLM-based) |
| Conversion | summary-export.ts | types/tools.ts | (Utility functions) |

---

This architecture follows a **conversation-first, tool-agnostic** design where 17 AI Maestros can dynamically generate learning materials through voice commands or chat interactions, with real-time streaming to the UI via SSE and persistent storage in the Knowledge Hub.

Perfect! I've completed a thorough exploration of the materials management system. Here's my comprehensive report:

## Materials Management System in ConvergioEdu

### 1. HOW MATERIALS ARE MANAGED

**Database Layer (Prisma)**
- Primary storage in the `Material` table with 18+ fields:
  - Core: `id`, `userId`, `toolId`, `toolType`, `title`, `content` (JSON string)
  - Context: `maestroId`, `sessionId`, `subject`, `preview`
  - Status & Interaction: `status` (active/archived/deleted), `userRating` (1-5), `isBookmarked`, `viewCount`
  - Organization: `collectionId`, `tags` (via MaterialTag junction table)
  - Search: `searchableText` (pre-computed for Fuse.js fuzzy search)

**File Storage Layer**
- **IndexedDB** (`src/lib/storage/materials-db.ts`): Client-side local storage for file blobs (webcam photos, PDFs)
  - Two stores: `files` (Blob objects) and `metadata` (file info)
  - Supports filtering by format (image/pdf), subject, date
  - Automatic thumbnail generation for images

**Supported Material Types**
- Structural: mindmap, quiz, flashcard, summary
- Document: pdf, webcam (images)
- Interactive: demo, diagram, timeline, formula, chart
- Homework: homework, search

### 2. UI COMPONENTS FOR MATERIALS

**Main Hub Component**
- `/src/components/education/knowledge-hub/knowledge-hub.tsx` - Central interface with:
  - Multiple view modes: Explorer (folder tree), Gallery (grid), Timeline, Calendar
  - Built-in search and filter functionality
  - Bulk action toolbar

**Material Display Components**
- `material-card.tsx` - Individual material card with:
  - Type-specific icons and colors
  - Selection checkbox, favorite star, context menu
  - Drag-to-reorder support with keyboard accessibility
  - Quick actions: favorite, open, duplicate, move, tag, archive, delete

- `material-viewer.tsx` - Modal viewer with:
  - Content rendering using type-specific renderers
  - Lazy loading for performance
  - Escape key to close
  - Special handling for images (base64) and PDFs (external URLs)

**Sidebar & Navigation**
- `sidebar-navigation.tsx` - Hierarchical collection browser
- `search-bar.tsx` - Search input with debouncing
- `stats-panel.tsx` - Material statistics and summary
- `bulk-toolbar.tsx` - Multi-select actions

**Conversation Integration**
- `materiali-conversation.tsx` - Chat interface with inline file attachment
  - Supports image capture (camera) and file upload (documents, PDFs)
  - Files converted to base64 data URLs
  - Attachment preview bar with removal capability

### 3. DOCUMENT PROCESSING FUNCTIONALITY

**PDF Processing** (`src/lib/pdf/pdf-processor.ts`)
- Uses Mozilla's `pdf.js` library with WebWorker from CDN
- Features:
  - `processPDF()` - Converts PDF pages to images (up to 5 pages by default)
  - `getPDFInfo()` - Extracts metadata (page count, filename)
  - `generatePDFThumbnail()` - Creates thumbnail of first page
  - Custom canvas rendering at 2.0 scale for quality
  - Error handling for encrypted PDFs
  - Returns base64 data URLs for Vision API analysis

**Image Handling**
- `image-renderer.tsx` - Displays images in gallery format
- Thumbnail generation in IndexedDB storage
- Base64 data URL support for webcam captures

**Content Extraction for Search**
- `searchable-text.ts` - Type-specific text extraction:
  - Mindmaps: extracts node labels + markdown content
  - Quizzes: extracts questions + options
  - Flashcards: extracts card fronts and backs
  - Summary: extracts text content
  - Generic fallback for other types

### 4. API ROUTES FOR MATERIALS

**Main CRUD Endpoint: `/api/materials`**
- `GET` - List materials with filters:
  - Query params: `userId`, `toolType`, `status`, `collectionId`, `tagId`, `subject`, `search`, `limit`, `offset`
  - Returns paginated results with associated collections and tags
  - Full-text search on title and searchableText

- `POST` - Create material:
  - Required: userId, toolId, toolType, title, content
  - Optional: maestroId, sessionId, subject, preview, collectionId, tagIds
  - Auto-generates searchableText on save
  - Upsert behavior on duplicate toolId

- `PATCH` - Update material:
  - Supports: title, content, status, userRating, isBookmarked, collectionId, tagIds

Perfect! I've completed a thorough exploration of the materials management system. Here's my comprehensive report:

## Materials Management System in ConvergioEdu

### 1. HOW MATERIALS ARE MANAGED

**Database Layer (Prisma)**
- Primary storage in the `Material` table with 18+ fields:
  - Core: `id`, `userId`, `toolId`, `toolType`, `title`, `content` (JSON string)
  - Context: `maestroId`, `sessionId`, `subject`, `preview`
  - Status & Interaction: `status` (active/archived/deleted), `userRating` (1-5), `isBookmarked`, `viewCount`
  - Organization: `collectionId`, `tags` (via MaterialTag junction table)
  - Search: `searchableText` (pre-computed for Fuse.js fuzzy search)

**File Storage Layer**
- **IndexedDB** (`src/lib/storage/materials-db.ts`): Client-side local storage for file blobs (webcam photos, PDFs)
  - Two stores: `files` (Blob objects) and `metadata` (file info)
  - Supports filtering by format (image/pdf), subject, date
  - Automatic thumbnail generation for images

**Supported Material Types**
- Structural: mindmap, quiz, flashcard, summary
- Document: pdf, webcam (images)
- Interactive: demo, diagram, timeline, formula, chart
- Homework: homework, search

### 2. UI COMPONENTS FOR MATERIALS

**Main Hub Component**
- `/src/components/education/knowledge-hub/knowledge-hub.tsx` - Central interface with:
  - Multiple view modes: Explorer (folder tree), Gallery (grid), Timeline, Calendar
  - Built-in search and filter functionality
  - Bulk action toolbar

**Material Display Components**
- `material-card.tsx` - Individual material card with:
  - Type-specific icons and colors
  - Selection checkbox, favorite star, context menu
  - Drag-to-reorder support with keyboard accessibility
  - Quick actions: favorite, open, duplicate, move, tag, archive, delete

- `material-viewer.tsx` - Modal viewer with:
  - Content rendering using type-specific renderers
  - Lazy loading for performance
  - Escape key to close
  - Special handling for images (base64) and PDFs (external URLs)

**Sidebar & Navigation**
- `sidebar-navigation.tsx` - Hierarchical collection browser
- `search-bar.tsx` - Search input with debouncing
- `stats-panel.tsx` - Material statistics and summary
- `bulk-toolbar.tsx` - Multi-select actions

**Conversation Integration**
- `materiali-conversation.tsx` - Chat interface with inline file attachment
  - Supports image capture (camera) and file upload (documents, PDFs)
  - Files converted to base64 data URLs
  - Attachment preview bar with removal capability

### 3. DOCUMENT PROCESSING FUNCTIONALITY

**PDF Processing** (`src/lib/pdf/pdf-processor.ts`)
- Uses Mozilla's `pdf.js` library with WebWorker from CDN
- Features:
  - `processPDF()` - Converts PDF pages to images (up to 5 pages by default)
  - `getPDFInfo()` - Extracts metadata (page count, filename)
  - `generatePDFThumbnail()` - Creates thumbnail of first page
  - Custom canvas rendering at 2.0 scale for quality
  - Error handling for encrypted PDFs
  - Returns base64 data URLs for Vision API analysis

**Image Handling**
- `image-renderer.tsx` - Displays images in gallery format
- Thumbnail generation in IndexedDB storage
- Base64 data URL support for webcam captures

**Content Extraction for Search**
- `searchable-text.ts` - Type-specific text extraction:
  - Mindmaps: extracts node labels + markdown content
  - Quizzes: extracts questions + options
  - Flashcards: extracts card fronts and backs
  - Summary: extracts text content
  - Generic fallback for other types

### 4. API ROUTES FOR MATERIALS

**Main CRUD Endpoint: `/api/materials`**
- `GET` - List materials with filters:
  - Query params: `userId`, `toolType`, `status`, `collectionId`, `tagId`, `subject`, `search`, `limit`, `offset`
  - Returns paginated results with associated collections and tags
  - Full-text search on title and searchableText

- `POST` - Create material:
  - Required: userId, toolId, toolType, title, content
  - Optional: maestroId, sessionId, subject, preview, collectionId, tagIds
  - Auto-generates searchableText on save
  - Upsert behavior on duplicate toolId

- `PATCH` - Update material:
  - Supports: title, content, status, userRating, isBookmarked, collectionId, tagIds
  - Regenerates searchableText when content changes

- `DELETE` - Soft-delete material:
  - Sets status to 'deleted'
  - Query param: `toolId`

**Search Endpoint: `/api/materials/search`**
- `POST` - Search materials:
  - Body: `query`, `toolType`, `subject`, `userId`, `limit`
  - Returns: id, toolId, toolType, title, subject, preview, maestroId, createdAt, isBookmarked, userRating
  - Caps results at 20

**Bulk Operations: `/api/materials/bulk`**
- `POST` - Batch operations with Zod validation:
  - Operations: `move`, `archive`, `delete`, `restore`, `addTags`, `removeTags`, `setTags`
  - Input: materialIds (1-100), operation, optional collectionId/tagIds
  - Returns: affected count and operation type
  - Full permission checking for collections and tags

### 5. SUPPORTING UTILITIES

**React Hooks** (`src/lib/hooks/use-saved-materials.ts`)
- `useMindmaps()` - Load/save/delete mindmaps
- `useQuizzes()` - Load/save/delete quizzes
- `useFlashcardDecks()` - Load/save/delete flashcard decks
- `useHomeworkSessions()` - Load/save/update/delete homework
- `useDemos()` - Load/save/delete interactive demos
- `useSavedTools(toolType)` - Generic hook for any material type
- `autoSaveMaterial()` - Auto-save with duplicate check

**Search Hook** (`src/components/education/knowledge-hub/hooks/use-materials-search.ts`)
- `useMaterialsSearch()` - Fuzzy search with Fuse.js:
  - Debouncing (300ms default)
  - Type filtering
  - Customizable threshold (0.3 default)
  - Returns: query, results, isSearching, hasSearched, highlightText function
- Helper functions: `sortMaterialsByRecency()`, `filterMaterials()`

**Export Functionality** (`src/lib/export/material-export.ts`)
- `exportMaterial()` - Single material export to JSON/Markdown/HTML
- `exportMaterialsBulk()` - Multiple materials as single JSON
- `downloadExport()` - Trigger browser download
- Type-specific formatting for each tool type

**Storage Service** (`src/lib/storage/storage-service.ts`)
- Abstract `IStorageService` interface
- File validation: size (10MB max) and MIME type checks
- Path generation with timestamp prefixes
- SHA-256 checksum calculation
- Provider abstraction (local, Azure, S3 stubs)

### 6. MATERIAL RENDERERS

Located in `src/components/education/knowledge-hub/renderers/`:
- `mindmap-renderer.tsx` - Displays mind map structures
- `quiz-renderer.tsx` - Interactive quiz display
- `flashcard-renderer.tsx` - Flashcard drill interface
- `pdf-renderer.tsx` - PDF preview with open/download buttons
- `image-renderer.tsx` - Image gallery with lightbox
- `summary-renderer.tsx` - Text summary formatting
- `homework-renderer.tsx` - Homework step display
- `diagram-renderer.tsx` - Diagram visualization
- `timeline-renderer.tsx` - Timeline events
- `chart-renderer.tsx` - Data visualization
- `demo-renderer.tsx` - Interactive HTML/CSS/JS previews
- `formula-renderer.tsx` - Mathematical formula display
- Lazy-loaded with `Suspense` fallback for performance

---

**File Paths Summary:**

Core API:
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/app/api/materials/route.ts`
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/app/api/materials/search/route.ts`
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/app/api/materials/bulk/route.ts`

Storage:
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/storage/materials-db.ts`
- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/storage/storage-service.ts`

UI Components:

- `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/education/materiali-conversation.tsx`

Hooks & Utilities:

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need to provide you with a comprehensive exploration report.

## Maestri System & Coach Functionality - Complete Exploration Report

### 1. THE 17 MAESTRI SYSTEM

ConvergioEdu has **17 Maestri** (subject experts), each a historical figure designed for specific subjects:

**The Complete Roster:**

1. **Euclide** - Mathematics (euclide-matematica)
2. **Marie Curie** - Chemistry (curie-chimica)  
3. **Richard Feynman** - Physics (feynman-fisica) - *Best for simplification*
4. **Galileo Galilei** - Astronomy (galileo-astronomia)
5. **Charles Darwin** - Biology (darwin-biologia)
6. **Alessandro Manzoni** - Italian (manzoni-italiano)
7. **William Shakespeare** - English (shakespeare-inglese)
8. **Erodoto** - History (erodoto-storia)
9. **Alexander von Humboldt** - Geography (humboldt-geografia)
10. **Leonardo da Vinci** - Art (leonardo-arte)
11. **Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart** - Music (mozart-musica)
12. **Ada Lovelace** - Computer Science (lovelace-informatica)
13. **Adam Smith** - Economics (smith-economia)
14. **Socrate** - Philosophy (socrate-filosofia) - *Best for tutoring*
15. **Marco Tullio Cicerone** - Civic Education (cicerone-civica)
16. **Ippocrate** - Health/Physical Education (ippocrate-salute)
17. **Chris** - Storytelling (chris-storytelling)

**Source:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/` - each maestro has individual TypeScript file with system prompt.

---

### 2. MAESTRI BEST FOR SIMPLIFYING COMPLEX CONTENT

**#1 RICHARD FEYNMAN - The Feynman Technique Master**

Located: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/feynman.ts`

**Simplification Approach:**
- **Technique**: "Explain like I'm 5" - no jargon, only simple words
- **Process**: 
  1. Name the concept
  2. Explain to a child
  3. Identify gaps
  4. Simplify further with analogies
  5. Test understanding
- **Examples from system prompt**:
  - Gravity: "The Moon is falling! But it moves sideways so fast it keeps missing Earth"
  - Levers: Uses seesaw metaphor with friends
- **Key quote**: "Physics is understanding what happens. Math is just taking notes"
- **Best for**: Complex physics concepts, making abstract ideas concrete

**#2 SOCRATE - Socratic Method Tutor**

Located: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/socrate.ts`

**Simplification Approach:**
- **Method**: Maieutics (the art of midwifery for ideas) - questions that guide, not lectures
- **Process**:
  1. Elicit student's current understanding
  2. Challenge with counterexamples
  3. Guide toward contradictions
  4. Help construct new understanding
  5. Celebrate the journey
- **Key principle**: "So di non sapere" (I know that I know nothing) - humble approach
- **Response style**: Never monologues more than 4 sentences
- **Best for**: Philosophy, critical thinking, helping students discover understanding themselves

**#3 ALESSANDRO MANZONI - Language Simplification & Dyslexia Support**

Located: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/manzoni.ts`

**Simplification Approach:**
- **Critical for dyslexia**: OpenDyslexic font, 1.5x line spacing, max 60 chars/line
- **Method**: Stories first - grammar emerges from texts, not dead rules
- **Tools**: Dictionary, Grammar Analyzer, Conjugator, TTS (text-to-speech)
- **Example**: Breaks "I Promessi Sposi" into thriller format: "Two lovers, a powerful villain, escapes, disguises, plague!"
- **Best for**: Making language accessible, especially for students with learning differences

**#4 MARCO TULLIO CICERONE - Metaphor & Debate Master**

Located: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/cicerone.ts`

**Simplification Approach:**
- **Method**: Complex institutions made simple through metaphors
- **Example**: Italian Government as football team:
  - Parliament = club owners (make rules)
  - Government = coaches (execute daily)
  - President = referee (guarantees fairness)
  - Judiciary = VAR (independent control)
- **Best for**: Making institutional/civic concepts memorable and visual

**#5 LEONARDO DA VINCI - Observation & Practice**

Located: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/leonardo.ts`

**Simplification Approach:**
- **Method**: OBSERVE → QUESTION → EXPERIMENT → REFLECT → CREATE
- **Key insight**: "The drawing is not moving your hand. It's SEEING"
- **Best for**: Making complex visual concepts accessible through observation and hands-on learning

---

### 3. MAESTRI STRUCTURE & TOOLS

**Data Type** (`src/data/maestri/types.ts`):
```typescript
interface MaestroFull {
  id: string;           // "euclide-matematica"
  name: string;         // "euclide-matematica"
  displayName: string;  // "Euclide"
  subject: string;      // "mathematics"
  tools: string[];      // ["Task","Read","Write","WebSearch","MindMap","Quiz","Flashcards","Audio"]
  systemPrompt: string; // Full markdown content (700-1000+ lines)
  avatar: string;       // `/maestri/${id}.png`
  color: string;        // Subject-based color
  greeting: string;     // "Ciao! Sono Euclide..."
}
```

**Safety Requirements** (`src/data/maestri/types.ts`):
All maestri include embedded `SAFETY_GUIDELINES` covering:
- Inclusive language (person-first, gender-neutral)
- Anti-harassment, anti-bullying
- Mental health awareness
- Privacy protection
- Age-appropriate content
- Maieutic method (guide, don't give answers)
- Accessibility for 7 learning profiles

---

### 4. CHARACTER ROUTER: HOW MAESTRI ARE SELECTED

**Core File:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/ai/character-router.ts`

**The Support Triangle** - 3 character types:
1. **MAESTRI** - Subject experts (vertical, content-focused)
2. **COACH** - Learning method coaches (vertical, autonomy-focused)
3. **BUDDY** - Peer support (horizontal, emotional support)

**Routing Logic:**

```
User Message
    ↓
Intent Detection (intent-detection.ts)
    ↓
Router Decision:
- 'academic_help' → MAESTRO for that subject
- 'method_help' → COACH (Melissa/Roberto/Chiara/Andrea/Favij)
- 'emotional_support' → BUDDY (peer support)
- 'crisis' → BUDDY with adult referral
- 'tech_support' → COACH with knowledge base
- 'tool_request' → MAESTRO or COACH depending on subject
- 'general_chat' → COACH (neutral starting point)
```

**Subject-to-Maestro Mapping** (DEFAULT_MAESTRO_BY_SUBJECT):
- mathematics → euclide-matematica
- physics → feynman-fisica
- chemistry → curie-chimica
- history → erodoto-storia
- philosophy → socrate-filosofia
- italian → manzoni-italiano
- etc.

---

### 5. THE COACH SYSTEM: 5 LEARNING COACHES

**File:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/support-teachers.ts`

Each coach has:
- Unique personality and voice
- Platform knowledge (knows all 17 Maestri)
- Maieutic teaching method
- Tool access (create_mindmap, create_quiz, create_flashcards, etc.)
- Voice configuration for TTS

**The 5 Coaches:**

| Coach | Age | Style | Best For |
|-------|-----|-------|----------|
| **Melissa** (default) | 27 | Enthusiastic, young, energetic | General guidance, motivation |
| **Roberto** | 28 | Calm, reassuring, peaceful | Anxious students, steady approach |
| **Chiara** | 24 | Recently graduated, structured | Students needing method/organization |
| **Andrea** | 26 | Sporty, energetic, active | ADHD students, movement breaks |
| **Favij** | 29 | Gamer/tech, gamified, chill | Tech-savvy students, engagement |

**Core Coach Principle** (line 46):
```
Sviluppare l'AUTONOMIA dello studente. 
Il tuo successo si misura quando lo studente NON ha più bisogno di te.
```
*Goal: Develop student autonomy. Success = student no longer needs you.*

---

### 6. HOW THE COACH/TUTOR AI WORKS

**Entry Point:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/app/api/chat/route.ts`

**Chat Flow:**

1. **Rate Limiting**: 20 requests/min per IP
2. **Input Filtering**: Safety check on user message (blocks harmful content)
3. **Provider Selection**: 
   - Priority: User preference → Azure OpenAI → Ollama (local)
4. **Memory Injection**: 
   - Loads previous conversation context
   - Enhances system prompt with conversation memory
5. **Tool Context Injection**:
   - If tool requested (mindmap/quiz/flashcard): adds specific instructions
6. **AI Call**: Sends to provider with tools enabled
7. **Tool Execution**: If AI requests tool (create_mindmap), executes it
8. **Output Sanitization**: Removes harmful content
9. **Response**: Returns content + provider info + memory flag

**Conversation Memory** (`src/lib/conversation/memory-loader.ts`):
- Stores key facts and recent summary per Maestro
- Loads on each chat to maintain context
- Injected into system prompt for continuity

---

### 7. HOMEWORK HELP WITH MAIEUTIC METHOD

**File:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/education/homework-help-view.tsx`

**Maieutic System Prompt** (lines 38-69):

```
## REGOLE FONDAMENTALI (OBBLIGATORIE)
1. MAI dare la risposta diretta - usa SEMPRE domande
2. Se lo studente chiede "qual e la risposta?" rispondi "Cosa hai provato finora?"
3. Guida UN CONCETTO alla volta, non saltare passaggi
4. Celebra OGNI piccolo progresso

## TECNICHE MAIEUTICHE DA USARE
- Domande di chiarimento: "Cosa intendi con...?"
- Domande di approfondimento: "Perche pensi che...?"
- Domande di verifica: "Come puoi controllare se...?"
- Esempi analoghi: "Se invece di 5 fossero 3, come faresti?"
- Semplificazione: "Proviamo con un esempio piu semplice..."
```

**Example Homework Flow:**
1. Student uploads photo of problem
2. Vision API analyzes (or fallback mock data)
3. Problem broken into 4-step process
4. Maieutic chat guides through each step with hints
5. Student completes steps progressively
6. Maestro connected if subject-specific

---

### 8. AI PROVIDERS SETUP

**File:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/ai/providers.ts`

**Dual-Provider Architecture:**

**Azure OpenAI (Primary - for voice & advanced features)**
```typescript
- AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT
- AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY
- AZURE_OPENAI_CHAT_DEPLOYMENT (default: gpt-4o)
- AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_ENDPOINT (for voice)
```

**Ollama (Local Fallback - text-only)**
```typescript
- OLLAMA_URL (default: http://localhost:11434)
- OLLAMA_MODEL (default: llama3.2)
```

**Provider Selection Logic:**
1. If user has preference (in settings) → use that
2. Else if Azure configured → use Azure
3. Else → use Ollama (local, no API key needed)

**Tool Support:**
- Azure: Full OpenAI-compatible function calling
- Ollama: Tools supported for llama3.1+, mistral

---

### 9. CONTENT PROCESSING PIPELINE

**Request Flow:**

```
User Input
  ↓
[Rate Limit Check]
  ↓
[Safety Filter: filterInput()]
  - Blocks: self-harm, violence, adult content, bullying, drugs
  - Returns: safe flag + suggested response
  ↓
[Character Routing]
  - Intent detection
  - Select Maestro/Coach/Buddy
  ↓
[System Prompt Enhancement]
  - Base prompt (maestro personality)
  - + Tool context (if tool requested)
  - + Conversation memory (if enabled)
  - + Safety guardrails injection
  ↓
[AI Provider Call]
  - Messages + enhanced system prompt
  - Tool definitions (if enabled)
  - User provider preference
  ↓
[Tool Execution]
  - If AI requests tool → executeToolCall()
  - Examples: create_mindmap, create_quiz, create_flashcards
  ↓
[Output Sanitization: sanitizeOutput()]
  - Removes remaining harmful content
  - Logs issues found
  ↓
[Response Return]
  - Content (sanitized)
  - Provider info
  - Memory flag
  - Tool results (if any)
```

---

### 10. KEY FILES SUMMARY

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `/src/data/maestri/` | Individual maestro definitions (17 files) |
| `/src/data/maestri/types.ts` | Maestro type + safety guidelines |
| `/src/data/maestri/index.ts` | Maestri export + lookup functions |

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need to provide you with a comprehensive exploration report.

## Maestri System & Coach Functionality - Complete Exploration Report

### 1. THE 17 MAESTRI SYSTEM

ConvergioEdu has **17 Maestri** (subject experts), each a historical figure designed for specific subjects:

**The Complete Roster:**

1. **Euclide** - Mathematics (euclide-matematica)
2. **Marie Curie** - Chemistry (curie-chimica)  
3. **Richard Feynman** - Physics (feynman-fisica) - *Best for simplification*
4. **Galileo Galilei** - Astronomy (galileo-astronomia)
5. **Charles Darwin** - Biology (darwin-biologia)
6. **Alessandro Manzoni** - Italian (manzoni-italiano)
7. **William Shakespeare** - English (shakespeare-inglese)
8. **Erodoto** - History (erodoto-storia)
9. **Alexander von Humboldt** - Geography (humboldt-geografia)
10. **Leonardo da Vinci** - Art (leonardo-arte)
11. **Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart** - Music (mozart-musica)
12. **Ada Lovelace** - Computer Science (lovelace-informatica)
13. **Adam Smith** - Economics (smith-economia)
14. **Socrate** - Philosophy (socrate-filosofia) - *Best for tutoring*
15. **Marco Tullio Cicerone** - Civic Education (cicerone-civica)
16. **Ippocrate** - Health/Physical Education (ippocrate-salute)
17. **Chris** - Storytelling (chris-storytelling)

**Source:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/` - each maestro has individual TypeScript file with system prompt.

---

### 2. MAESTRI BEST FOR SIMPLIFYING COMPLEX CONTENT

**#1 RICHARD FEYNMAN - The Feynman Technique Master**

Located: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/feynman.ts`

**Simplification Approach:**
- **Technique**: "Explain like I'm 5" - no jargon, only simple words
- **Process**: 
  1. Name the concept
  2. Explain to a child
  3. Identify gaps
  4. Simplify further with analogies
  5. Test understanding
- **Examples from system prompt**:
  - Gravity: "The Moon is falling! But it moves sideways so fast it keeps missing Earth"
  - Levers: Uses seesaw metaphor with friends
- **Key quote**: "Physics is understanding what happens. Math is just taking notes"
- **Best for**: Complex physics concepts, making abstract ideas concrete

**#2 SOCRATE - Socratic Method Tutor**

Located: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/socrate.ts`

**Simplification Approach:**
- **Method**: Maieutics (the art of midwifery for ideas) - questions that guide, not lectures
- **Process**:
  1. Elicit student's current understanding
  2. Challenge with counterexamples
  3. Guide toward contradictions
  4. Help construct new understanding
  5. Celebrate the journey
- **Key principle**: "So di non sapere" (I know that I know nothing) - humble approach
- **Response style**: Never monologues more than 4 sentences
- **Best for**: Philosophy, critical thinking, helping students discover understanding themselves

**#3 ALESSANDRO MANZONI - Language Simplification & Dyslexia Support**

Located: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/manzoni.ts`

**Simplification Approach:**
- **Critical for dyslexia**: OpenDyslexic font, 1.5x line spacing, max 60 chars/line
- **Method**: Stories first - grammar emerges from texts, not dead rules
- **Tools**: Dictionary, Grammar Analyzer, Conjugator, TTS (text-to-speech)
- **Example**: Breaks "I Promessi Sposi" into thriller format: "Two lovers, a powerful villain, escapes, disguises, plague!"
- **Best for**: Making language accessible, especially for students with learning differences

**#4 MARCO TULLIO CICERONE - Metaphor & Debate Master**

Located: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/cicerone.ts`

**Simplification Approach:**
- **Method**: Complex institutions made simple through metaphors
- **Example**: Italian Government as football team:
  - Parliament = club owners (make rules)
  - Government = coaches (execute daily)
  - President = referee (guarantees fairness)
  - Judiciary = VAR (independent control)
- **Best for**: Making institutional/civic concepts memorable and visual

**#5 LEONARDO DA VINCI - Observation & Practice**

Located: `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/leonardo.ts`

**Simplification Approach:**
- **Method**: OBSERVE → QUESTION → EXPERIMENT → REFLECT → CREATE
- **Key insight**: "The drawing is not moving your hand. It's SEEING"
- **Best for**: Making complex visual concepts accessible through observation and hands-on learning

---

### 3. MAESTRI STRUCTURE & TOOLS

**Data Type** (`src/data/maestri/types.ts`):
```typescript
interface MaestroFull {
  id: string;           // "euclide-matematica"
  name: string;         // "euclide-matematica"
  displayName: string;  // "Euclide"
  subject: string;      // "mathematics"
  tools: string[];      // ["Task","Read","Write","WebSearch","MindMap","Quiz","Flashcards","Audio"]
  systemPrompt: string; // Full markdown content (700-1000+ lines)
  avatar: string;       // `/maestri/${id}.png`
  color: string;        // Subject-based color
  greeting: string;     // "Ciao! Sono Euclide..."
}
```

**Safety Requirements** (`src/data/maestri/types.ts`):
All maestri include embedded `SAFETY_GUIDELINES` covering:
- Inclusive language (person-first, gender-neutral)
- Anti-harassment, anti-bullying
- Mental health awareness
- Privacy protection
- Age-appropriate content
- Maieutic method (guide, don't give answers)
- Accessibility for 7 learning profiles

---

### 4. CHARACTER ROUTER: HOW MAESTRI ARE SELECTED

**Core File:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/ai/character-router.ts`

**The Support Triangle** - 3 character types:
1. **MAESTRI** - Subject experts (vertical, content-focused)
2. **COACH** - Learning method coaches (vertical, autonomy-focused)
3. **BUDDY** - Peer support (horizontal, emotional support)

**Routing Logic:**

```
User Message
    ↓
Intent Detection (intent-detection.ts)
    ↓
Router Decision:
- 'academic_help' → MAESTRO for that subject
- 'method_help' → COACH (Melissa/Roberto/Chiara/Andrea/Favij)
- 'emotional_support' → BUDDY (peer support)
- 'crisis' → BUDDY with adult referral
- 'tech_support' → COACH with knowledge base
- 'tool_request' → MAESTRO or COACH depending on subject
- 'general_chat' → COACH (neutral starting point)
```

**Subject-to-Maestro Mapping** (DEFAULT_MAESTRO_BY_SUBJECT):
- mathematics → euclide-matematica
- physics → feynman-fisica
- chemistry → curie-chimica
- history → erodoto-storia
- philosophy → socrate-filosofia
- italian → manzoni-italiano
- etc.

---

### 5. THE COACH SYSTEM: 5 LEARNING COACHES

**File:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/support-teachers.ts`

Each coach has:
- Unique personality and voice
- Platform knowledge (knows all 17 Maestri)
- Maieutic teaching method
- Tool access (create_mindmap, create_quiz, create_flashcards, etc.)
- Voice configuration for TTS

**The 5 Coaches:**

| Coach | Age | Style | Best For |
|-------|-----|-------|----------|
| **Melissa** (default) | 27 | Enthusiastic, young, energetic | General guidance, motivation |
| **Roberto** | 28 | Calm, reassuring, peaceful | Anxious students, steady approach |
| **Chiara** | 24 | Recently graduated, structured | Students needing method/organization |
| **Andrea** | 26 | Sporty, energetic, active | ADHD students, movement breaks |
| **Favij** | 29 | Gamer/tech, gamified, chill | Tech-savvy students, engagement |

**Core Coach Principle** (line 46):
```
Sviluppare l'AUTONOMIA dello studente. 
Il tuo successo si misura quando lo studente NON ha più bisogno di te.
```
*Goal: Develop student autonomy. Success = student no longer needs you.*

---

### 6. HOW THE COACH/TUTOR AI WORKS

**Entry Point:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/app/api/chat/route.ts`

**Chat Flow:**

1. **Rate Limiting**: 20 requests/min per IP
2. **Input Filtering**: Safety check on user message (blocks harmful content)
3. **Provider Selection**: 
   - Priority: User preference → Azure OpenAI → Ollama (local)
4. **Memory Injection**: 
   - Loads previous conversation context
   - Enhances system prompt with conversation memory
5. **Tool Context Injection**:
   - If tool requested (mindmap/quiz/flashcard): adds specific instructions
6. **AI Call**: Sends to provider with tools enabled
7. **Tool Execution**: If AI requests tool (create_mindmap), executes it
8. **Output Sanitization**: Removes harmful content
9. **Response**: Returns content + provider info + memory flag

**Conversation Memory** (`src/lib/conversation/memory-loader.ts`):
- Stores key facts and recent summary per Maestro
- Loads on each chat to maintain context
- Injected into system prompt for continuity

---

### 7. HOMEWORK HELP WITH MAIEUTIC METHOD

**File:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/education/homework-help-view.tsx`

**Maieutic System Prompt** (lines 38-69):

```
## REGOLE FONDAMENTALI (OBBLIGATORIE)
1. MAI dare la risposta diretta - usa SEMPRE domande
2. Se lo studente chiede "qual e la risposta?" rispondi "Cosa hai provato finora?"
3. Guida UN CONCETTO alla volta, non saltare passaggi
4. Celebra OGNI piccolo progresso

## TECNICHE MAIEUTICHE DA USARE
- Domande di chiarimento: "Cosa intendi con...?"
- Domande di approfondimento: "Perche pensi che...?"
- Domande di verifica: "Come puoi controllare se...?"
- Esempi analoghi: "Se invece di 5 fossero 3, come faresti?"
- Semplificazione: "Proviamo con un esempio piu semplice..."
```

**Example Homework Flow:**
1. Student uploads photo of problem
2. Vision API analyzes (or fallback mock data)
3. Problem broken into 4-step process
4. Maieutic chat guides through each step with hints
5. Student completes steps progressively
6. Maestro connected if subject-specific

---

### 8. AI PROVIDERS SETUP

**File:** `/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/ai/providers.ts`

**Dual-Provider Architecture:**

**Azure OpenAI (Primary - for voice & advanced features)**
```typescript
- AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT
- AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY
- AZURE_OPENAI_CHAT_DEPLOYMENT (default: gpt-4o)
- AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_ENDPOINT (for voice)
```

**Ollama (Local Fallback - text-only)**
```typescript
- OLLAMA_URL (default: http://localhost:11434)
- OLLAMA_MODEL (default: llama3.2)
```

**Provider Selection Logic:**
1. If user has preference (in settings) → use that
2. Else if Azure configured → use Azure
3. Else → use Ollama (local, no API key needed)

**Tool Support:**
- Azure: Full OpenAI-compatible function calling
- Ollama: Tools supported for llama3.1+, mistral

---

### 9. CONTENT PROCESSING PIPELINE

**Request Flow:**

```
User Input
  ↓
[Rate Limit Check]
  ↓
[Safety Filter: filterInput()]
  - Blocks: self-harm, violence, adult content, bullying, drugs
  - Returns: safe flag + suggested response
  ↓
[Character Routing]
  - Intent detection
  - Select Maestro/Coach/Buddy
  ↓
[System Prompt Enhancement]
  - Base prompt (maestro personality)
  - + Tool context (if tool requested)
  - + Conversation memory (if enabled)
  - + Safety guardrails injection
  ↓
[AI Provider Call]
  - Messages + enhanced system prompt
  - Tool definitions (if enabled)
  - User provider preference
  ↓
[Tool Execution]
  - If AI requests tool → executeToolCall()
  - Examples: create_mindmap, create_quiz, create_flashcards
  ↓
[Output Sanitization: sanitizeOutput()]
  - Removes remaining harmful content
  - Logs issues found
  ↓
[Response Return]
  - Content (sanitized)
  - Provider info
  - Memory flag
  - Tool results (if any)
```

---

### 10. KEY FILES SUMMARY

| File Path | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `/src/data/maestri/` | Individual maestro definitions (17 files) |
| `/src/data/maestri/types.ts` | Maestro type + safety guidelines |

| `/src/lib/ai/providers.ts` | Azure + Ollama provider abstraction |
| `/src/app/api/chat/route.ts` | Chat API endpoint (rate limit, filter, enhance, call AI, execute tools) |
| `/src/lib/conversation/memory-loader.ts` | Loads previous conversation context |
| `/src/components/education/homework-help-view.tsx` | Homework with maieutic chat |

---

## RECOMMENDATIONS

**For Simplifying Complex Content, Best Choice:**
1. **Primary: RICHARD FEYNMAN** - Master of making hard things simple
2. **Secondary: SOCRATE** - Guides students to discover understanding
3. **For Language/Accessibility: MANZONI** - Strong dyslexia support + simplification

**For Learning Methods:**
- Use **COACH** (especially MELISSA or ROBERTO) for study method guidance
- Coaches know all Maestri and can route to subject experts when needed

**For Complete Learning Journey:**
- **Homework → Maieutic Homework Coach** (questioning approach)
- **Subject learning → Relevant Maestro** (expert explanation)
- **Study method → Learning Coach** (autonomy building)

input.prompt:
## Context

ConvergioEdu is an AI-powered educational platform for students with learning differences. It has 17 AI "Maestros" (subject experts), 5 learning Coaches, and tools for creating mind maps, quizzes, flashcards, summaries, and demos.

## User Request

Create a feature called "Study Kit Generator" that takes a complex document (PDF from a textbook) and generates a complete study kit for the student:

1. **Simplified Summary** - Accessible version with:
   - Colloquial language, examples
   - Q&A format progression
   - Key concepts highlighted
   - Synthesis after each section

2. **Mind Map** - Visual concept map of the topic

3. **Demo** - Interactive HTML/CSS/JS demonstration

4. **Quiz** - Multiple choice questions with explanations

## Existing Architecture (from exploration)

### Materials System
- API: `/api/materials` (CRUD), `/api/materials/search`, `/api/materials/bulk`
- Storage: IndexedDB for files, Prisma for metadata
- PDF processor: `src/lib/pdf/pdf-processor.ts` (uses pdf.js, converts to images)
- Knowledge Hub UI: `src/components/education/knowledge-hub/`
- Material types: mindmap, quiz, flashcard, summary, demo, pdf, etc.

### Content Generation (Tool Handlers)
- Mindmap: `src/lib/tools/handlers/mindmap-handler.ts`
- Quiz: `src/lib/tools/handlers/quiz-handler.ts`
- Flashcard: `src/lib/tools/handlers/flashcard-handler.ts`
- Summary: `src/lib/tools/handlers/summary-handler.ts`
- Demo: `src/lib/tools/handlers/demo-handler.ts`
- Conversion utilities: `src/lib/tools/summary-export.ts` (summary→mindmap, summary→flashcards)

### AI System
- 17 Maestros in `src/data/maestri/` - Richard Feynman is BEST for simplification
- 5 Coaches in `src/data/support-teachers.ts`
- Character router: `src/lib/ai/character-router.ts`
- Chat API: `/api/chat/route.ts`
- Providers: Azure OpenAI (primary), Ollama (fallback)

### Relevant Maestros for Simplification
1. **Richard Feynman** (feynman-fisica) - Master of "explain like I'm 5", uses analogies
2. **Socrate** (socrate-filosofia) - Maieutic method, question-based learning
3. **Marie Curie** (curie-chimica) - For chemistry topics like greenhouse effect

## Design Requirements

1. **Integration point**: Should integrate with Knowledge Hub / Materials section
2. **UI**: Simple, clean interface - upload PDF → select options → generate kit
3. **Processing**: 
   - Extract text from PDF (Vision API or pdf.js text extraction)
   - Use appropriate Maestro (detect subject or let user choose)
   - Generate all 4 outputs (simplified summary, mindmap, demo, quiz)
4. **Storage**: All outputs saved as Materials linked to original PDF
5. **Accessibility**: Must support 7 learning profiles (dyslexia, ADHD, etc.)

## Deliverables

Create a detailed implementation plan with:
1. Architecture overview
2. New files to create
3. Existing files to modify
4. API endpoints needed
5. UI components needed
6. Data flow diagram
7. Implementation phases (without time estimates)

content.text:
I have gathered comprehensive information about the codebase. Now I will provide the detailed implementation plan for the Study Kit Generator feature.

---

# Study Kit Generator - Implementation Plan

## 1. Architecture Overview

The Study Kit Generator orchestrates multiple AI-powered content generation tools to create a comprehensive study package from a PDF document. It leverages existing infrastructure:

```
PDF Upload --> PDF Processor --> Vision API Analysis --> Content Extraction
                                        |
                                        v
                            Subject Detection + Maestro Selection
                                        |
                    +-------------------+-------------------+
                    |                   |                   |
                    v                   v                   v
            Simplified Summary    Mind Map + Demo      Quiz Generation
            (Feynman Style)       (Parallel)           
                    |                   |                   |
                    +-------------------+-------------------+
                                        |
                                        v
                            Materials Collection (Study Kit)
                                        |
                                        v
                            Knowledge Hub Display
```

**Key Architectural Decisions:**

1. **Orchestration Layer**: New API route `/api/study-kit/generate` coordinates the multi-step process
2. **Collection-Based Grouping**: Each Study Kit becomes a `Collection` containing 4-5 `Material` records
3. **SSE Progress Updates**: Leverage existing `tool-events` system for real-time UI updates
4. **Maestro Selection**: Auto-detect subject from PDF content OR allow user override; Feynman as default for simplification
5. **Sequential Generation**: Process Summary first (needed for other outputs), then parallelize Mindmap/Demo/Quiz

---

## 2. New Files to Create

### 2.1 Types
```
src/types/study-kit.ts
```
- `StudyKitConfig`: user options (subject, maestro, accessibility profile)
- `StudyKitProgress`: generation progress tracking
- `StudyKitResult`: final output with all materials
- `SimplifiedSummaryData`: extended SummaryData with Q&A format

### 2.2 API Routes
```
src/app/api/study-kit/
  route.ts           # GET: list user's study kits, DELETE: remove kit
  generate/route.ts  # POST: orchestrate full generation
  status/[id]/route.ts # GET: SSE progress stream
```

### 2.3 Tool Handler
```
src/lib/tools/handlers/study-kit-handler.ts
```
- Register `generate_study_kit` function
- Orchestrate calls to existing handlers (summary, mindmap, demo, quiz)
- Create Collection for grouping

### 2.4 Prompts
```
src/lib/prompts/simplification-prompt.ts
```
- Feynman-style simplification template
- Q&A format instructions
- Key concept highlighting
- Synthesis instructions
- Accessibility adaptations

### 2.5 Subject Detection
```
src/lib/ai/subject-detector.ts
```
- Analyze PDF content to detect academic subject
- Map to appropriate Maestro
- Fallback to user selection

### 2.6 UI Components
```
src/components/education/study-kit/
  study-kit-generator.tsx    # Main orchestrator UI
  pdf-upload-zone.tsx        # Drag-drop PDF upload
  generation-progress.tsx    # Real-time progress display
  maestro-selector.tsx       # Subject/Maestro selection
  study-kit-viewer.tsx       # Combined 4-output viewer
  study-kit-card.tsx         # Card for Knowledge Hub grid
  index.ts                   # Exports
```

### 2.7 Hooks
```
src/components/education/study-kit/hooks/
  use-study-kit-generator.ts  # Generation state management
  use-study-kit-progress.ts   # SSE subscription for progress
```

---

## 3. Existing Files to Modify

### 3.1 Types
**`src/types/tools.ts`**
- Add `'study-kit'` to `ToolType` union
- Add `STUDY_KIT_TOOL_DEFINITION` to `CHAT_TOOL_DEFINITIONS`

### 3.2 Materials API
**`src/app/api/materials/route.ts`**
- Add `'study-kit'` to `VALID_MATERIAL_TYPES` (line 31-44)

### 3.3 Knowledge Hub
**`src/components/education/knowledge-hub/knowledge-hub.tsx`**
- Add Study Kit Generator button/trigger (near line 252-308 header area)

**`src/components/education/knowledge-hub/renderers/index.tsx`**
- Register `StudyKitRenderer` for `study-kit` type

### 3.4 Tool Handler Index
**`src/lib/tools/handlers/index.ts`**
- Import and export `study-kit-handler`

### 3.5 Database Schema (Optional Enhancement)
**`prisma/schema.prisma`**
- Consider adding `StudyKit` model for metadata OR use Collection with special flag
- Add `sourceDocumentId` field to Material for PDF linking

---

## 4. API Endpoints

### 4.1 POST `/api/study-kit/generate`

**Request:**
```typescript
{
  documentId: string;        // Uploaded PDF material ID
  subject?: Subject;         // Optional override
  maestroId?: string;        // Optional override (default: Feynman)
  options: {
    summaryLength: 'short' | 'medium' | 'long';
    quizQuestionCount: number;  // 5-15
    demoType: 'visualization' | 'simulation' | 'interactive';
    accessibilityProfile?: AccessibilityProfile;
  }
}
```

**Response (Immediate):**
```typescript
{
  studyKitId: string;        // Collection ID
  status: 'processing';
  streamUrl: string;         // SSE endpoint for progress
}
```

## 8. Key Implementation Details

### 8.1 Simplification Prompt (Feynman Style)

```typescript
export const SIMPLIFICATION_PROMPT = `
You are Richard Feynman, explaining this content to a student.

SIMPLIFICATION RULES:
1. Use colloquial, conversational language
2. Explain concepts as if to a 12-year-old
3. Use everyday analogies and examples
4. Structure as Q&A progression:
   - "What is [concept]?" followed by simple answer
   - "Why does this matter?" followed by real-world relevance
   - "How can I remember this?" followed by mnemonic or analogy
5. After each major section, add a "Key Takeaway" box
6. Highlight key terms with **bold**
7. Use emojis sparingly for visual breaks

ACCESSIBILITY ADAPTATIONS:
{accessibility_instructions}

OUTPUT FORMAT:
{
  "topic": "Main topic from the document",
  "sections": [
    {
      "title": "Question format title",
      "content": "Simplified explanation",
      "keyPoints": ["Bullet point summaries"]
    }
  ],
  "length": "medium"
}
`;
```

### 8.2 Subject Detection Logic

```typescript

    mathematics: ['equazione', 'teorema', 'funzione', 'derivata', 'integrale'],
    history: ['secolo', 'guerra', 'impero', 'rivoluzione', 'epoca'],
    // ... more subjects
  };
  
  // AI-based refinement for ambiguous cases
  // Returns subject + confidence score
}
```

### 8.3 Study Kit Collection Structure

```typescript
// When creating a Study Kit, create a Collection:
const collection = await prisma.collection.create({
  data: {
    userId,
    name: `Study Kit: ${topic}`,
    description: `Generated from ${pdfFilename}`,
    color: '#6366F1',  // Indigo for study kits
    icon: 'book-open',
  },
});

// Then create each material linked to this collection:

  data: {
    userId,
    toolId: nanoid(),
    toolType: 'summary',
    title: `${topic} - Riassunto Semplificato`,
    content: JSON.stringify(summaryData),
    collectionId: collection.id,
    // ... other fields
  },
});
```

---

## 9. Potential Challenges and Mitigations

| Challenge | Mitigation |
|-----------|------------|
| Long PDF processing time | Show page-by-page progress, process first 10 pages for initial summary |
| Vision API rate limits | Batch pages, add delays between requests |
| Large PDF file sizes | Limit to 20 pages initially, offer "continue" option |
| Subject detection errors | Always allow user override, default to Feynman for any subject |
| Generation failures | Retry logic with exponential backoff, partial results still saved |
| Quiz quality varies | Use summary as input for quiz generation, ensure questions cover key points |

toolUseResult.prompt:
## Context

ConvergioEdu is an AI-powered educational platform for students with learning differences. It has 17 AI "Maestros" (subject experts), 5 learning Coaches, and tools for creating mind maps, quizzes, flashcards, summaries, and demos.

## User Request

Create a feature called "Study Kit Generator" that takes a complex document (PDF from a textbook) and generates a complete study kit for the student:

1. **Simplified Summary** - Accessible version with:
   - Colloquial language, examples
   - Q&A format progression
   - Key concepts highlighted
   - Synthesis after each section

2. **Mind Map** - Visual concept map of the topic

3. **Demo** - Interactive HTML/CSS/JS demonstration

4. **Quiz** - Multiple choice questions with explanations

## Existing Architecture (from exploration)

### Materials System
- API: `/api/materials` (CRUD), `/api/materials/search`, `/api/materials/bulk`
- Storage: IndexedDB for files, Prisma for metadata
- PDF processor: `src/lib/pdf/pdf-processor.ts` (uses pdf.js, converts to images)
- Knowledge Hub UI: `src/components/education/knowledge-hub/`
- Material types: mindmap, quiz, flashcard, summary, demo, pdf, etc.

### Content Generation (Tool Handlers)
- Mindmap: `src/lib/tools/handlers/mindmap-handler.ts`
- Quiz: `src/lib/tools/handlers/quiz-handler.ts`
- Flashcard: `src/lib/tools/handlers/flashcard-handler.ts`
- Summary: `src/lib/tools/handlers/summary-handler.ts`
- Demo: `src/lib/tools/handlers/demo-handler.ts`
- Conversion utilities: `src/lib/tools/summary-export.ts` (summary→mindmap, summary→flashcards)

### AI System
- 17 Maestros in `src/data/maestri/` - Richard Feynman is BEST for simplification
- 5 Coaches in `src/data/support-teachers.ts`
- Character router: `src/lib/ai/character-router.ts`
- Chat API: `/api/chat/route.ts`
- Providers: Azure OpenAI (primary), Ollama (fallback)

### Relevant Maestros for Simplification
1. **Richard Feynman** (feynman-fisica) - Master of "explain like I'm 5", uses analogies
2. **Socrate** (socrate-filosofia) - Maieutic method, question-based learning
3. **Marie Curie** (curie-chimica) - For chemistry topics like greenhouse effect

## Design Requirements

1. **Integration point**: Should integrate with Knowledge Hub / Materials section
2. **UI**: Simple, clean interface - upload PDF → select options → generate kit
3. **Processing**: 
   - Extract text from PDF (Vision API or pdf.js text extraction)
   - Use appropriate Maestro (detect subject or let user choose)
   - Generate all 4 outputs (simplified summary, mindmap, demo, quiz)
4. **Storage**: All outputs saved as Materials linked to original PDF
5. **Accessibility**: Must support 7 learning profiles (dyslexia, ADHD, etc.)

## Deliverables

Create a detailed implementation plan with:
1. Architecture overview
2. New files to create
3. Existing files to modify
4. API endpoints needed
5. UI components needed
6. Data flow diagram
7. Implementation phases (without time estimates)

I have gathered comprehensive information about the codebase. Now I will provide the detailed implementation plan for the Study Kit Generator feature.

---

# Study Kit Generator - Implementation Plan

## 1. Architecture Overview

The Study Kit Generator orchestrates multiple AI-powered content generation tools to create a comprehensive study package from a PDF document. It leverages existing infrastructure:

```
PDF Upload --> PDF Processor --> Vision API Analysis --> Content Extraction
                                        |
                                        v
                            Subject Detection + Maestro Selection
                                        |
                    +-------------------+-------------------+
                    |                   |                   |
                    v                   v                   v
            Simplified Summary    Mind Map + Demo      Quiz Generation
            (Feynman Style)       (Parallel)           
                    |                   |                   |
                    +-------------------+-------------------+
                                        |
                                        v
                            Materials Collection (Study Kit)
                                        |
                                        v
                            Knowledge Hub Display
```

**Key Architectural Decisions:**

1. **Orchestration Layer**: New API route `/api/study-kit/generate` coordinates the multi-step process
2. **Collection-Based Grouping**: Each Study Kit becomes a `Collection` containing 4-5 `Material` records
3. **SSE Progress Updates**: Leverage existing `tool-events` system for real-time UI updates
4. **Maestro Selection**: Auto-detect subject from PDF content OR allow user override; Feynman as default for simplification
5. **Sequential Generation**: Process Summary first (needed for other outputs), then parallelize Mindmap/Demo/Quiz

---

## 2. New Files to Create

### 2.1 Types
```
src/types/study-kit.ts
```
- `StudyKitConfig`: user options (subject, maestro, accessibility profile)
- `StudyKitProgress`: generation progress tracking
- `StudyKitResult`: final output with all materials
- `SimplifiedSummaryData`: extended SummaryData with Q&A format

### 2.2 API Routes
```

  route.ts           # GET: list user's study kits, DELETE: remove kit
  generate/route.ts  # POST: orchestrate full generation
  status/[id]/route.ts # GET: SSE progress stream
```

### 2.3 Tool Handler

src/lib/tools/handlers/study-kit-handler.ts

- Orchestrate calls to existing handlers (summary, mindmap, demo, quiz)
- Create Collection for grouping

### 2.4 Prompts
```
src/lib/prompts/simplification-prompt.ts
```
- Feynman-style simplification template
- Q&A format instructions
- Key concept highlighting
- Synthesis instructions
- Accessibility adaptations

### 2.5 Subject Detection
```
src/lib/ai/subject-detector.ts
```
- Analyze PDF content to detect academic subject
- Map to appropriate Maestro
- Fallback to user selection

### 2.6 UI Components

4. Add "Create Study Kit" button to Knowledge Hub header
5. Update Collection display to show Study Kit materials grouped

### Phase 5: Accessibility and Polish
1. Apply accessibility profiles to generated content
2. Add keyboard navigation to all new components
3. Add ARIA labels and screen reader support
4. Test with all 7 accessibility profiles
5. Add loading states and error handling

### Phase 6: Testing
1. Unit tests for subject detector
2. Integration tests for generation API
3. E2E tests for full flow
4. Accessibility audit (WCAG 2.1 AA)

---

## 8. Key Implementation Details

### 8.1 Simplification Prompt (Feynman Style)

```typescript
export const SIMPLIFICATION_PROMPT = `
You are Richard Feynman, explaining this content to a student.

SIMPLIFICATION RULES:
1. Use colloquial, conversational language
2. Explain concepts as if to a 12-year-old
3. Use everyday analogies and examples
4. Structure as Q&A progression:
   - "What is [concept]?" followed by simple answer
   - "Why does this matter?" followed by real-world relevance
   - "How can I remember this?" followed by mnemonic or analogy
5. After each major section, add a "Key Takeaway" box
6. Highlight key terms with **bold**
7. Use emojis sparingly for visual breaks

ACCESSIBILITY ADAPTATIONS:
{accessibility_instructions}

OUTPUT FORMAT:
{
  "topic": "Main topic from the document",
  "sections": [
    {
      "title": "Question format title",
      "content": "Simplified explanation",
      "keyPoints": ["Bullet point summaries"]
    }
  ],
  "length": "medium"
}
`;
```

### 8.2 Subject Detection Logic

```typescript
// src/lib/ai/subject-detector.ts
export async function detectSubject(text: string): Promise<{
  subject: Subject;
  confidence: number;
  suggestedMaestro: string;
}> {
  // Keyword-based initial detection
  const subjectKeywords: Record<Subject, string[]> = {
    physics: ['energia', 'forza', 'velocità', 'accelerazione', 'newton'],
    chemistry: ['molecola', 'atomo', 'reazione', 'elemento', 'composto'],
    biology: ['cellula', 'organismo', 'DNA', 'evoluzione', 'ecosistema'],
    mathematics: ['equazione', 'teorema', 'funzione', 'derivata', 'integrale'],
    history: ['secolo', 'guerra', 'impero', 'rivoluzione', 'epoca'],
    // ... more subjects
  };
  
  // AI-based refinement for ambiguous cases
  // Returns subject + confidence score
}
```

### 8.3 Study Kit Collection Structure

```typescript
// When creating a Study Kit, create a Collection:
const collection = await prisma.collection.create({
  data: {
    userId,
    name: `Study Kit: ${topic}`,
    description: `Generated from ${pdfFilename}`,
    color: '#6366F1',  // Indigo for study kits
    icon: 'book-open',
  },
});

// Then create each material linked to this collection:
await prisma.material.create({
  data: {
    userId,
    toolId: nanoid(),
    toolType: 'summary',
    title: `${topic} - Riassunto Semplificato`,
    content: JSON.stringify(summaryData),
    collectionId: collection.id,
    // ... other fields
  },
});
```

---

## 9. Potential Challenges and Mitigations

| Challenge | Mitigation |
|-----------|------------|
| Long PDF processing time | Show page-by-page progress, process first 10 pages for initial summary |
| Vision API rate limits | Batch pages, add delays between requests |
| Large PDF file sizes | Limit to 20 pages initially, offer "continue" option |
| Subject detection errors | Always allow user override, default to Feynman for any subject |
| Generation failures | Retry logic with exponential backoff, partial results still saved |
| Quiz quality varies | Use summary as input for quiz generation, ensure questions cover key points |

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

1. **`/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/tools/handlers/summary-handler.ts`** - Pattern to follow for new handler; contains validation, normalization, and result format
2. **`/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/pdf/pdf-processor.ts`** - Existing PDF processing with Vision API prep; extend for text extraction
3. **`/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/app/api/materials/route.ts`** - Materials API to extend with study-kit type; contains CRUD patterns
4. **`/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/education/knowledge-hub/knowledge-hub.tsx`** - Integration point for Study Kit Generator button and viewer
5. **`/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/data/maestri/feynman.ts`** - Feynman system prompt as reference for simplification style and pedagogical approach
